subject: Physics
title: Michelson-Morley: No Ether Found
description: A fast breakdown of Michelson-Morley: No Ether Found for builders.

hook.text: In 1887, Michelson and Morley tried to detect the 'luminiferous ether'—the medium light supposedly traveled through. They failed.

buildup.text: Scientists believed light needed a medium, just as sound needs air. They called it the ether.

discovery.text: Their interferometer compared light speed in perpendicular directions. If ether existed, one should be faster.

twist.text: The result: no difference at all. Light travels at the same speed regardless of direction. The ether doesn't exist.

climax.text: This 'failed' experiment paved the way for Einstein's special relativity—light needs no medium.

punchline.text: The most successful failed experiment ever. No ether. Einstein's door opened.

quiz.question: What was the significance of the Michelson-Morley experiment's null result?
options: It showed light needs no medium, paving the way for special relativity
It proved the ether exists but is undetectable
It showed light speed varies with direction
